user_text: create me a complete lesson that explain the concept of "disctionary" in python. it should be for beginners level

messages.content:
You are an expert autonomous Task Orchestrator.
Your goal is to analyze user requests and educational materials, then create a structured execution plan.

Available Agents:
- 'coder': Generates runnable Python code. Best for demonstrations, data analysis, and coding tutorials.
- 'video': Creates video content. Best for visual explanations.
- 'text': Generates markdown text/notes. Best for summaries, lesson plans, and articles.
- 'quizzer': Creates quizzes. Best for assessments and review.

Instructions:
1. Analyze the user's input and provided files.
2. Break down the goal into logical subtasks.
3. Assign each subtask to the most appropriate agent.
4. Provide specific, actionable instructions for each agent.
5. For every subtask, include a non-empty 'acceptance_criteria' list.
   - Each criterion must include: criterion_id, criterion_type, target, operator, expected, severity.
   - Use criterion_type values: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic.
   - Use operator values: equals | contains | exists | gte | lte.
   - Use target paths that start with 'result.' or 'tools.'.
   - For mcp_tool criteria, include tool: { name, args }.
   - Use placeholders like $artifacts[0] or $output.validation.path inside tool args.
6. Use the provided tool 'create_task_plan' to submit your plan.

Response Format Example:
{
  "user_intent": "Create a lesson on Python lists with a video intro and a quiz.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "task_id": "step1_code",
      "agent": "coder",
      "instruction": "Generate a Python script demonstrating list operations.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "code_executes",
          "criterion_type": "mcp_tool",
          "tool": {"name": "python_check", "args": {"filepath": "$artifacts[0]"}},
          "target": "tools.python_check.success",
          "operator": "equals",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "tools.python_check.run.stderr"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "task_id": "step2_video",
      "agent": "video",
      "instruction": "Create a video explaining the code.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": ["step1_code"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "video_semantic_ok",
          "criterion_type": "semantic",
          "target": "result.output",
          "operator": "exists",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "result.output"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

function.description: Create a structured execution plan for multiple agents.

type.description: Criterion type: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic
type.title: Type

target.description: Evaluation target path (e.g., result.output.text or tools.python_check.success)

operator.title: Operator

tool.description: Tool call definition for mcp_tool criteria

evidence.description: Evidence path to include in judger output

agent.description: The agent responsible (coder, video, text, quizzer)
agent.title: Agent

dependencies.description: IDs of tasks that must complete first
dependencies.title: Dependencies

acceptance_criteria.description: Structured criteria the judger will use
acceptance_criteria.title: Acceptance Criteria

user_intent.description: Summary of what the user wants to achieve
user_intent.title: User Intent

Ensure all examples run without external dependencies and include expected output as comments where helpful.","dependencies":["text_lesson_markdown"],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"code_block_exists","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"```python","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"covers_nested","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"nested","severity":"optional","evidence":"result.output"}]},{"id":"quiz_beginner_dictionary","agent":"quizzer","title":"Create a short beginner quiz (10 questions) on Python dictionaries: multiple choice + a few code-reading questions + 1 short coding prompt. Provide an answer key with brief explanations.","dependencies":["text_lesson_markdown"],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"ten_questions","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"1.","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"answer_key_present","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"Answer key","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"}]}]}

parsed_plan.user_intent: Create a complete beginner-level lesson explaining Python dictionaries.

subtasks.instruction: Write a complete beginner-friendly lesson in Markdown explaining Python dictionaries. Include: learning objectives; what a dictionary is (mapping key→value); syntax/creation; accessing values; adding/updating/removing; common methods (get, keys, values, items, pop, update); looping patterns; nested dictionaries; practical examples; common mistakes (KeyError, mutable values, key types); mini-exercises with solutions; summary and further practice prompts.

Provide runnable Python code snippets used in the lesson (dictionary creation, CRUD, loops, nested dict, methods) consolidated into a single script-like block. Ensure all examples run without external dependencies and include expected output as comments where helpful.
Create a short beginner quiz (10 questions) on Python dictionaries: multiple choice + a few code-reading questions + 1 short coding prompt. Provide an answer key with brief explanations.

final_plan.user_intent: Create a complete beginner-level lesson explaining Python dictionaries.

Provide runnable Python code snippets used in the lesson (dictionary creation, CRUD, loops, nested dict, methods) consolidated into a single script-like block. Ensure all examples run without external dependencies and include expected output as comments where helpful.
Create a short beginner quiz (10 questions) on Python dictionaries: multiple choice + a few code-reading questions + 1 short coding prompt. Provide an answer key with brief explanations.

output.text:
Generated text content based on: The submission is only a placeholder and does not provide the requested beginner-friendly Markdown lesson on Python dictionaries.

To fix:
1. Replace the placeholder with a full Markdown lesson.
2. Include a clearly titled section: "Learning objectives".
3. Explain what a dictionary is as a mapping key→value.
4. Cover dictionary syntax/creation with code examples.
5. Show accessing values (indexing and `get()`), explicitly including code that contains `get(`.
6. Demonstrate adding, updating, and removing entries.
7. Include a section on common methods: `get`, `keys`, `values`, `items`, `pop`, `update` with examples.
8. Show looping patterns (over keys, values, items) with examples.
9. Explain nested dictionaries with examples.
10. Provide practical examples (e.g., counting words, configuration, lookup table).
11. Include common mistakes and how to avoid them: `KeyError`, mutable values pitfalls, and valid key types.
12. Add an "Exercises" section containing mini-exercises with solutions.
13. Finish with a summary and further practice prompts.

quiz_beginner_dictionary.instruction: Provide a complete beginner quiz with exactly 10 questions on Python dictionaries, including multiple-choice, a few code-reading questions, and 1 short coding prompt. Format the output so it clearly includes numbered questions (e.g., '1.', '2.', … '10.') and include an 'Answer key' section with brief explanations for each question.

output.code:
# Dictionary Operations - Complete Tutorial

# 1. Dictionary Creation
print("=== Dictionary Creation ===")
# Creating empty dictionary
empty_dict = {}
print(f"Empty dictionary: {empty_dict}")

# Creating dictionary with data
student = {
    "name": "Alice",
    "age": 20,
    "grade": "A",
    "courses": ["Math", "Physics"]
}
print(f"Student dictionary: {student}")

# Using dict() constructor
person = dict(name="Bob", age=25, city="New York")
print(f"Person dictionary: {person}")

# 2. CRUD Operations
print("\n=== CRUD Operations ===")
# Create - Adding new key-value pairs
student["email"] = "alice@university.edu"
print(f"After adding email: {student}")

# Read - Accessing values
print(f"Student name: {student['name']}")
print(f"Student age: {student.get('age')}")
print(f"Non-existent key with default: {student.get('height', 'Not specified')}")

# Update - Modifying existing values
student["age"] = 21
student["grade"] = "A+"
print(f"After updating: {student}")

# Delete - Removing key-value pairs
del student["courses"]
print(f"After deleting courses: {student}")

# Using pop()
email = student.pop("email")
print(f"Popped email: {email}")
print(f"After popping email: {student}")

# 3. Looping Through Dictionaries
print("\n=== Looping Through Dictionaries ===")
# Loop through keys
print("Keys only:")
for key in student:
    print(f"  {key}")

# Loop through values
print("Values only:")
for value in student.values():
    print(f"  {value}")

# Loop through key-value pairs
print("Key-value pairs:")
for key, value in student.items():
    print(f"  {key}: {value}")

# 4. Nested Dictionaries
print("\n=== Nested Dictionaries ===")
# Creating nested dictionary
classroom = {
    "class1": {
        "teacher": "Dr. Smith",
        "students": {
            "alice": {"age": 20, "grade": "A"},
            "bob": {"age": 22, "grade": "B"}
        }
    },
    "class2": {
        "teacher": "Prof. Johnson",
        "students": {
            "charlie": {"age": 19, "grade": "A+"},
            "diana": {"age": 21, "grade": "B+"}
        }
    }
}

print("Classroom structure:")
print(classroom)

# Accessing nested values
print(f"Class1 teacher: {classroom['class1']['teacher']}")
print(f"Alice's grade: {classroom['class1']['students']['alice']['grade']}")

# Modifying nested values
classroom["class1"]["students"]["alice"]["grade"] = "A+"
print(f"Updated Alice's grade: {classroom['class1']['students']['alice']['grade']}")

# 5. Dictionary Methods
print("\n=== Dictionary Methods ===")
# Copy dictionary
student_copy = student.copy()
print(f"Copy of student: {student_copy}")

# Clear dictionary
student.clear()
print(f"After clearing student: {student}")

# Fromkeys method
keys = ['a', 'b', 'c']
default_value = 0
new_dict = dict.fromkeys(keys, default_value)
print(f"Fromkeys result: {new_dict}")

# Get method with default
print(f"Using get with default: {new_dict.get('d', 'Not found')}")

# Items, keys, values methods
sample_dict = {'x': 1, 'y': 2, 'z': 3}
print(f"All items: {sample_dict.items()}")
print(f"All keys: {sample_dict.keys()}")
print(f"All values: {sample_dict.values()}")

# Update method
sample_dict.update({'w': 4, 'x': 10})  # Updates existing key, adds new one
print(f"After update: {sample_dict}")

# Popitem method (removes and returns last inserted item)
last_item = sample_dict.popitem()
print(f"Popped item: {last_item}")
print(f"After popitem: {sample_dict}")

# Setdefault method
print(f"Setdefault result: {sample_dict.setdefault('a', 5)}")
print(f"After setdefault: {sample_dict}")

# Expected Output:
"""
=== Dictionary Creation ===
Empty dictionary: {}
Student dictionary: {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 20, 'grade': 'A', 'courses': ['Math', 'Physics']}
Person dictionary: {'name': 'Bob', 'age': 25, 'city': 'New York'}

=== CRUD Operations ===
After adding email: {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 20, 'grade': 'A', 'courses': ['Math', 'Physics'], 'email': 'alice@university.edu'}
Student name: Alice
Student age: 20
Non-existent key with default: Not specified
After updating: {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 21, 'grade': 'A+', 'courses': ['Math', 'Physics'], 'email': 'alice@university.edu'}
After deleting courses: {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 21, 'grade': 'A+', 'email': 'alice@university.edu'}
Popped email: alice@university.edu
After popping email: {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 21, 'grade': 'A+'}

=== Looping Through Dictionaries ===
Keys only:
  name
  age
  grade
Values only:
  Alice
  21
  A+
Key-value pairs:
  name: Alice
  age: 21
  grade: A+

=== Nested Dictionaries ===
Classroom structure:
{'class1': {'teacher': 'Dr. Smith', 'students': {'alice': {'age': 20, 'grade': 'A'}, 'bob': {'age': 22, 'grade': 'B'}}}, 'class2': {'teacher': 'Prof. Johnson', 'students': {'charlie': {'age': 19, 'grade': 'A+'}, 'diana': {'age': 21, 'grade': 'B+'}}}}
Class1 teacher: Dr. Smith
Alice's grade: A
Updated Alice's grade: A+

=== Dictionary Methods ===
Copy of student: {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 21, 'grade': 'A+'}
After clearing student: {}
Fromkeys result: {'a': 0, 'b': 0, 'c': 0}
Using get with default: Not found
All items: dict_items([('x', 1), ('y', 2), ('z', 3)])
All keys: dict_keys(['x', 'y', 'z'])
All values: dict_values([1, 2, 3])
After update: {'x': 10, 'y': 2, 'z': 3, 'w': 4}
Popped item: ('w', 4)
After popitem: {'x': 10, 'y': 2, 'z': 3}
Setdefault result: 5
After setdefault: {'x': 10, 'y': 2, 'z': 3, 'a': 5}
"""

coder_snippets_validation.instruction: Provide runnable Python code snippets used in the lesson (dictionary creation, CRUD, loops, nested dict, methods) consolidated into a single script-like block. Ensure all examples run without external dependencies and include expected output as comments where helpful.
